name: Jim Himes
tweet: @chrisbrad22 @TEE1031 @Juliess31 @MEPFuller Advocating with your representative is not ludicrous. It is at the very core of our democracy. Are you arguing that the way forward here is totally obvious? What if someone out there disagrees with you?

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @FlyingMezerkis @MEPFuller Yes. I am a representative. And who do you think I represent? People. And don’t you think I (and all my colleagues) should be responsible about understanding what they (you) think?

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @kateinCT1 @Juliess31 @MEPFuller Well, threats are not the most persuasive form of argument, but if you read my tweets, you will see that I have spent the evening arguing for us to reflect on what our constituents want. Here and in swing states.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @BillyVonElds @RepDwightEvans And I’m sorry, play back for me what is disappointing to you. All I’ve said all night is that we should do some due diligence on public sentiment here. I’m kinda hung on that “Representative” part of my title.

tweet: @Juliess31 @MEPFuller Thought experiment: Let’s stipulate that Trump will not be removed by impeachment. And let’s stipulate the possibility that impeachment meaningfully increases his chance of re-election (something I don’t know right now). What do you do?

tweet: @ryannicholos @MEPFuller No. Which is why that particular function rests in the judiciary, not the legislature. But prosecutors make charge/no charge decisions every single day.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @MissingLynx @MEPFuller Not exactly what I’m saying. Historically, Congress has impeached, and will again. But nothing in the constitution forces Congress to impeach in a given circumstance. If you disagree, send me the language that forces Congress to do so.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @MEPFuller Where Matt, do I say it is “all political”? Seriously. I am a huge defender of the press. But it’s hard to do that when I take the time to engage with you and you mistate my argument. Or talk about my “look”. Are you trying to get at the truth here, or to stir the pot?

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @kylelitke @MEPFuller No. And I’m not arguing that.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @williamg1 @MEPFuller I know the facts. And again, I’m not arguing for doing nothing. I’m arguing for acting in a smart and considered way.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @forkinshirtball @MEPFuller I know Twitter is a universe where logic is not important, but I’m not arguing for doing nothing, I’m arguing for doing what we do in a fully informed way.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @pwnedlib @MEPFuller I think you’re not reading it very carefully.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @selina3sticks @MEPFuller As a non lawyer, I couldn’t agree more.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @MEPFuller There is no explicit duty as there is conduct a census every 10 years. Congress decides if and when to impeach independent of the words of the constitution. That’s not controversial. It’s true. So it’s not unreasonable to try to understand where American sentiment is in deciding.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @MEPFuller Lordy. It’s not about a look. Duty is in quotes because the constitution imposes absolutely no obligation to impeach under any circumstance. It’s at the discretion of Congress. Kinda feels like you’re looking for a lot more fireworks than you’re going to find in this quarter.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @MEPFuller Like everything else a legislator does, there’s a mix of “knowing” and what public sentiment demands. Especially in the House, where we are called “Representative”. Impeachment could have been granted to SCOTUS, but instead, it was given to the part of our gov closest to people.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @MEPFuller I don’t think there’s an opposition between the two in this case. Impeachment is by design a political act, not a judicial or legal one. So public sentiment is as essential (probably more) as any implicit constitutional “duty”.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @MEPFuller Anyway, I was making an argument for care and consideration of facts and implications in however we choose to act. I expressed no view on how we should act.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @MEPFuller I made the case, because along with the will of my constituents, that would be something I would want to know before developing intense conviction. You know, be as informed as possible before acting?

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @MEPFuller I don’t know what you heard, but I wasn’t forceful about not impeaching Trump (your words). I was forceful about making sure we had the best picture possible of how impeachment plays in Presidential swing states. Something I don’t know. See the diff?

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @MEPFuller Which is different than “tamping down” the possibility of impeachment.

name: Jim Himes
tweet: @MEPFuller No it wasn’t. Jim Himes argued that we should reflect on and consider the effect of impeachment proceedings on the chances of the President’s re-election, without prejudice. Because if he is not convicted by the Senate, 2020 is the next mechanism of accountability.
